headline: The recommended educators converge on harnessing and routing multiple coding models as interchangeable, governed agents.
body: Cole Medin presents Omnigent, a meta-harness that orchestrates Claude Code, Codex, and Pi as interchangeable workers, routing implementation and review tasks to different models with human-in-the-loop governance and cross-device sessions [2], while Cyril Imhof frames harness engineering and loops as the path for building AI applications in regulated industries, alongside open versus closed source and model shutdown risk [0]. IBM Technology reinforces the routing thread, reporting that labs now prioritize intelligent routing over raw model size for cost efficiency and trustworthiness [5], and details Kagenti's identity-based controls and delegation chains for securing multi-agent deployments [1], plus AI pair programming for debugging and code review [3]. On the inference side, Better Stack stress-tests Xiaomi and TileRT's 1-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model, which exceeds 1,000 tokens per second using selective FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and a persistent GPU kernel runtime [7]. Beyond the inner circle, Google Cloud Tech builds long-running, event-driven agents with the open source ADK [10], and AWS demonstrates Claude Code on Bedrock with prompt caching, cost tracking, and content guardrails for compliance [9].
